problem_text: Real GDP (in constant base-year dollars) was 2859.5 (billion dollars) in an early year and 13598.5 (billion dollars) in a later year. Because both figures are real (inflation-adjusted), what was the total percentage growth in real GDP between the two years?

content.meaning: real GDP is inflation-adjusted (valued in constant base-year prices), so a change in real GDP reflects a change in quantity of output, not a change in the price level

content.action: subtract the earlier real GDP g1 = 2859.5 from the later real GDP g2 = 13598.5 to get the absolute change in real GDP
content.purpose: isolate the real (quantity) change in output before converting it to a percentage

content.action: divide the change in real GDP by the earlier value g1 and multiply by 100 to express the real growth as a percentage
content.purpose: convert the absolute real change into the percentage growth in real output
